pillars.label: P2P / Transaction Operations
pillars.description: Requisition-to-approval-to-PO-to-invoice-to-payment operating flow, including vendor setup/validation and payment execution controls.
doneMeans: Workflow approvals are designed and system-enforced.
Vendor setup/validation path and payment flow ownership are explicit.
Key manual handoffs are reduced or intentionally controlled.
Exception handling and evidence retention paths are defined.
pillars.executivePrompt: Are we building a scalable, controlled transaction engine or recreating today's manual P2P bottlenecks?

pillars.label: Financial Reporting / Data Model
pillars.description: Close and reporting outputs, data model/COA/cost centers, multi-entity and multi-GAAP reporting, FP&A data access, and reporting extract quality.
doneMeans: Target COA/cost-center design (or explicit mapping strategy) is agreed.
Reporting extract/data model supports finance and FP&A needs.
Multi-entity, FX, and multi-GAAP requirements are explicitly designed.
Consultant dependency vs in-house reporting scope is a deliberate decision.
pillars.executivePrompt: Will the ERP materially improve reporting quality and reduce manual bridging, or only move transactions into a new UI?

pillars.label: SOX / Controls / Auditability
pillars.description: Control design, audit trail/evidence, remediation tracking, owner accountability, and system-enforced control capabilities.
doneMeans: Key controls are mapped to named owners and system/process steps.
Audit evidence retention paths are defined for critical flows.
Deficiency/remediation ownership and tracking repository are explicit.
ERP design decisions support testable and traceable controls.
pillars.executivePrompt: Does the ERP program close auditability gaps by design, or defer controls decisions until after selection?

crossCutting.label: Platform / Enablement
crossCutting.description: IT architecture, integrations, access, resourcing, change management, and execution feasibility supporting all three pillars.
